diagrams.title: Basic Bipartite Graph with Matching
diagrams.description: Shows a bipartite graph with two disjoint sets of vertices (U on the left, V on the right). Vertices U = {u1, u2, u3, u4} are on the left side, and V = {v1, v2, v3, v4} on the right side. Edges connect vertices across the two sets. A matching is shown with thicker, highlighted edges connecting u1-v1, u2-v3, and u3-v4, while u4 remains unmatched. This helps students visualize what constitutes a bipartite graph and a matching.
elements: left set of vertices U (u1,u2,u3,u4) arranged vertically
right set of vertices V (v1,v2,v3,v4) arranged vertically
edges between U and V showing possible connections
highlighted/bolder edges representing the matching (u1-v1, u2-v3, u3-v4)
labels for sets U and V
unmatched vertex u4 shown distinctly
diagrams.purpose: Introduce the fundamental structure of bipartite graphs and demonstrate what a matching looks like in practice

diagrams.title: Augmenting Path Demonstration
diagrams.description: Shows the same bipartite graph with an existing matching (u1-v1, u2-v3). An augmenting path is highlighted: starting from unmatched vertex u3, going through edge u3-v2 (unmatched), then v2-u1 (matched edge, traversed backward), then u1-v4 (unmatched), ending at unmatched vertex v4. The path alternates between unmatched and matched edges. Arrows indicate the direction of traversal, and a transformation arrow shows the resulting improved matching with u3-v2 and u1-v4 added.
elements: bipartite graph with two sets U and V
existing matching edges (u1-v1, u2-v3) shown in one color
unmatched edges shown in another color
augmenting path highlighted with distinct color and direction arrows
unmatched start/end vertices u3 and v4 emphasized
transformation showing before and after matching states
legend explaining matched vs unmatched edges
diagrams.purpose: Explain the concept of augmenting paths and how they are used to find larger matchings, a key concept in the augmenting path algorithm

diagrams.title: Maximum Matching Result
diagrams.description: Shows the final maximum matching in a complete bipartite graph example with U = {u1, u2, u3} and V = {v1, v2, v3}. All three vertices on each side are matched (u1-v1, u2-v2, u3-v3). The diagram uses color coding: green for matched vertices and edges, dashed gray for unused possible edges. A corner annotation shows 'Maximum Matching Size: 3' with a brief note about Hall's Marriage Theorem condition being satisfied.
elements: three vertices on left set U in green (matched)
three vertices on right set V in green (matched)
three thick green edges representing maximum matching
dashed gray lines showing non-matching possible edges
set labels U and V
annotation box showing 'Maximum Matching Size: 3'
visual indicator that all vertices are matched
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrate the end result of finding a maximum matching where no more augmenting paths exist, reinforcing the termination condition of matching algorithms
